key_themes: Strong analyst consensus (Strong Buy) with ~$130+ average price targets implying 25-35% upside
Raised 2026 EPS guidance ($12-14) and record demand for premium/corporate travel
Fuel cost pressures from oil volatility (recently easing with oil price drops and Strait reopening)
Merger speculation with American Airlines (high antitrust/regulatory skepticism; AAL rejected reports)
Earnings beats, capacity discipline, and long-term positioning despite high capex
summary: Wall Street remains strongly bullish on UAL with a Strong Buy rating and price targets centered around $130-131, driven by United's raised 2026 profit outlook, earnings beats, record bookings, and ability to offset fuel cost spikes through revenue growth and premium demand. Recent stock pops (trading ~$95-102 in mid-April 2026) were fueled by easing oil prices and initial merger rumors with American Airlines, though the latter faced immediate antitrust pushback and denial from AAL. While fuel volatility, high 2026 capex, and regulatory risks create some caution, the dominant narrative is one of resilient fundamentals and upside potential ahead of Q1 2026 earnings.
sources: 15+ recent web news articles (Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, MarketBeat, Reuters, CNBC, company IR), 20+ recent X posts (semantic search + keyword search for $UAL since April 2026), Analyst consensus from 16-17 firms (UBS, Citigroup, TD Cowen, etc.)
